gems: Eminox EMx S5 is first RAS-approved NRMM retrofit product in UK (Stage IIIA to Stage V) — Balfour Beatty VINCI saved GBP50M retrofitting 19 machines vs replacement; proven compliance pathway for Finland/Sweden to replicate
FASTWATER methanol retrofit kits (200 kW-4 MW) proven on harbour tug, pilot boat, coast guard vessel — lowest-risk entry point for ferry methanol conversion; kit already commercialised
Balfour Beatty VINCI HS2 retrofit programme is largest documented NRMM retrofit in UK — model for Skanska/NCC in Nordic markets where similar Stage V enforcement is emerging
Austrian hospital standby genset market (MITTES Engineering) — active tender pipeline with 300-800 kW units that match Proventia genset range; replicable in Finland/Sweden hospital sector
REISFER Interreg project (EUR 2024-2027) covers 4 ferry operators across Finland, Estonia, Sweden, Aland — direct warm introduction path for methanol retrofit
GreenPilot (313 kW methanol pilot boat, Swedish Maritime Administration) — directly references methanol retrofit kit vendors; established Swedish public sector reference

Predictive Maintenance search queries — confirmed zero leads via public search
pattern_learned: Marine ferry retrofit market is rapidly bifurcating: large vessels (>1 MW) are moving toward methanol/hydrogen new builds while smaller vessels (<800 kW) are prime for methanol retrofit kits or battery hybrid installs. Stationary power hospitals (300-800 kW range) represent the most actionable vertical — active tenders, short sales cycles, clear OEM dealer networks (kW-set, MITTES). UK NRMM is the most structurally developed retrofit market globally due to Stage V enforcement and the HS2 programme creating a documented GBP50M saving from retrofit vs replace. Finland/Sweden NRMM is currently dominated by electric new-build trends rather than retrofits. EU inland waterways are heavily hydrogen-funded (Horizon Europe, FLAGSHIPS, RH2IWER) — hydrogen ICE retrofit kits (like ULEMCo H2ICED) are the analogue to Proventia's methanol offering in this segment. Predictive Maintenance remains a dead end for lead generation via public search.
